D1.name: Nautical Dawn
D1.detail: The star's position when the sun is 12 degrees below the horizon before sunrise. At this time, stars brighter than magnitude 4 are visible to the naked eye in most parts of the sky.

D2.name: Civil Dawn
D2.detail: The star's position when the sun is 6 degrees below the horizon before sunrise. At this time, stars brighter than magnitude 1 are visible to the naked eye in most parts of the sky.

D3.name: Sunrise
D3.detail: The star's position at sunrise. At this time, only the brightest stars or planets, such as Venus and Jupiter, may be visible to the naked eye.

N1.name: Sunset
N1.detail: The star's position at sunset. At this time, only the brightest stars or planets, such as Venus and Jupiter, may be visible to the naked eye.

N2.name: Civil Dusk
N2.detail: The star's position when the sun is 6 degrees below the horizon after sunset. At this time, stars brighter than magnitude 1 are visible to the naked eye in most parts of the sky.

N3.name: Nautical Dusk
N3.detail: The star's position when the sun is 12 degrees below the horizon after sunset. At this time, stars brighter than magnitude 4 are visible to the naked eye in most parts of the sky.

R.name: Rising Point
R.detail: The point where the star appears to rise above the horizon.

T.name: Meridian Transit
T.detail: The point where the star transits the meridian.

S.name: Setting Point
S.detail: The point where the star appears to dip below the horizon.

line_legend.solid: The star's path in the nighttime sky.
line_legend.darkDashed: The star's path during nautical twilight.
line_legend.lightDashed: The star's path during civil twilight.
line_legend.dotted: The star's path in the daytime sky.

no_dst: The Standard Time and offset in the current time zone for this location, without Daylight Saving Time (DST).
